title: $VRT hiring surge and $15B backlog suggest AI infrastructure growth isn't slowing
author: CoolioBeansTTV
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/ytdi1ynlnuqg1.png?width=1092&format=png&auto=webp&s=2c90531c79b1423208aaf1daa1741000a5edcaef

  
While the market's debating whether AI spending is finally hitting a wall, Vertiv just posted hiring velocity that doesn't match that narrative. Over the last 180 days, their LinkedIn job openings doubled from 1,000 to 2,000 roles. That's not maintenance hiring -that's scaling for demand.

Here's what caught my attention: their Q4 order book jumped 252% YoY with a $15B backlog. Companies don't double their hiring and report that kind of order growth if they're bracing for a slowdown. They report it when they're struggling to meet demand.

2025 numbers back this up. Revenue hit $10.23B (+27.7% YoY), earnings grew 168%. They're guiding 30%+ revenue growth for 2026. The backlog alone gives them 18+ months of visibility.

The other signal: employee business outlook hit 85% positive (up significantly YoY). The people closest to the business aren't hedging. They believe this is real.

VRT is basically the plumbing for data centers and AI infrastructure -cooling, power, management systems. Everyone watches chip stocks, but someone has to actually build and operate the infrastructure. Their hiring velocity + backlog suggests they're not in any shortage of demand from that side.

Hiring velocity as a leading indicator feels like it's being slept on. What's your take -is this the kind of alt data signal that actually predicts forward revenue?

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body: Hiring and backlog are lagging indicators, especially in capex cycles where orders get pulled forward. VRT’s fine, but reading this as “AI spend isn’t slowing” feels like extrapolating one vendor a bit hard.

comments.author: gaana15
comments.body:
Google’s TurboQuant just greenlit a global density war: every "saved" dollar is a mandate for high density GPU racks.

As racks move from 50kW to 150kW+, Vertiv’s TAM will explode - it’s shifting from "optional cooling" to a mandatory, liquid-cooled upgrade cycle that traditional data centers can’t survive without.
